error_message: `npm run db:up` failed inside the Codex sandbox with `cannot create XDG_RUNTIME_DIR folder "/run/user/1000/snap.docker": read-only file system`.
root_cause: Docker needs to create runtime files under `/run/user`, which is blocked by the normal workspace sandbox.
fix: Reran `npm run db:up` with platform escalation; Docker pulled `pgvector/pgvector:pg16` and started `innerscript-postgres` plus `innerscript-postgres-test`.

error_message: `npm run db:migrate` failed with `sh: 1: drizzle-kit: not found`.
root_cause: The latest InnerScript checkout depends on Drizzle, but the existing `node_modules` did not contain the current dev dependencies after pulling the latest code.
fix: Started `npm install` to refresh dependencies before rerunning migrations.

error_message: `npm run db:migrate` spun at `applying migrations` and exited nonzero because `.env.local` pointed to `db:5432` with an old Docker-internal database URL.
root_cause: The current README/docker-compose local setup runs Drizzle from the host against `localhost:5433`, but `.env.local` still used an older container-only host URL.
fix: Changed local `DATABASE_URL` to `postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/innerscript`.

error_message: `npm run dev` started but Next.js warned that it inferred the workspace root from `/home/moneydrome/2026-Projects/2026-Active-Projects/package-lock.json` instead of the InnerScript repo.
root_cause: There are multiple lockfiles in the active-projects container, so Next.js may choose the parent workspace as the tracing root unless `outputFileTracingRoot` is set.
fix: Not fixed yet; server is running for UI review. Add `outputFileTracingRoot: process.cwd()` or the appropriate repo root setting in `next.config.js` after local review.

error_message: Joel could not create new notes from the sidebar.
root_cause: The New Note button only opened a local draft, and the API rejected empty bodies, so no note appeared in the sidebar until content existed.
fix: Allowed empty manual entry bodies, kept imported/source entries requiring content, and changed New Note to create/select a real blank note immediately.

error_message: `npm run build` failed with `EACCES: permission denied, unlink '.next/server/_instrument_lib_db_index_js.js'`.
root_cause: The generated `.next` file was owned by `nobody:nogroup`, so normal build cleanup could not unlink it. Next also warned that it inferred the parent active-projects root because multiple lockfiles exist.
fix: Set `turbopack.root = process.cwd()` in `next.config.js`; rerun build with platform escalation so generated `.next` output can be cleaned.

error_message: `rm -rf .next` failed with permission denied on generated build files owned by `nobody:nogroup`.
root_cause: The local generated Next.js output directory contains files and directories not owned by the workspace user, so this session cannot clean the build cache without host/root ownership repair.
fix: Production build remains locally blocked until `.next` is removed or chowned outside this restricted session; `npm test` passes and the dev server returns 200 OK.

error_message: Joel reported: title Enter should jump to body; New Note looked slow or broken; sidebar note title fell back to body text; sidebar note cards showed creation date instead of name only.
root_cause: The title input had no Enter key focus handoff, sidebar labels derived from the first body line when title was empty, sidebar cards rendered dates as visible secondary text, and New Note had no creating feedback while the blank entry request was in flight.
fix: Enter in the title now focuses the body textarea; empty titles stay `Untitled`; sidebar cards render only the name; New Note shows a disabled `Creating...` state while creating the real blank note.

error_message: Joel reported the saving status flashes too fast during autosave.
root_cause: The save indicator rendered raw `idle`, `saving`, and `saved` state changes directly, so fast autosave cycles created visible flicker. The first fix also triggered the React lint rule against synchronous setState inside effects.
fix: Added delayed display timing: `Saving...` appears only after a short delay, `Saved` stays readable briefly, and display state updates happen from timers to satisfy lint.

error_message: Joel reported the save status does not change at all for further edits after the first save cycle.
root_cause: The displayed save status depended mainly on repeated status strings, so repeated save cycles could collapse when React saw the same parent state and the internal display timer had already returned to idle.
fix: Added an explicit save activity counter and a `dirty` state. Every edit now increments activity, shows `Unsaved changes`, then proceeds through `Saving...` and `Saved`.

error_message: Storybook at `localhost:6006` showed `Oh no! Your Storybook is empty` after generated starter stories were removed and colocated component stories were added.
file: .storybook/main.js; package.json; local Storybook process on port 6006
root_cause: The Storybook dev process on port 6006 was stale. It started before the current colocated story glob and story files were in place, so its served `index.json` still had empty entries. The browser could also keep an old path to the deleted starter docs story.
fix: Restarted the Storybook process, verified `/index.json` listed the colocated `components/**/*.stories.jsx` stories, and updated the `storybook` script with `--initial-path /?path=/story/journal-entry-editor--written-entry` so fresh launches land on an existing InnerScript story.

error_message: First `npm test` after adding shadcn Collapsible failed because Vite re-optimized the new `@base-ui/react/collapsible` dependency and reloaded Storybook tests.
root_cause: The shadcn Collapsible component introduced a new Base UI import, so Vite optimized a new dependency during the test run and Storybook's dynamic test modules reloaded mid-run.
fix: Reran `npm test` after dependency optimization completed; all 35 tests passed. No config change made unless this repeats.

error_message: First `npm test` after adding shadcn Dropdown Menu failed because Vite re-optimized the new `@base-ui/react/menu` dependency and reloaded Storybook tests.
root_cause: The dropdown menu component introduced a new Base UI menu import, so Vite optimized a new dependency during the browser test run and Storybook's dynamic modules reloaded mid-run.
fix: Added `@base-ui/react/menu` to `.storybook/main.js` `optimizeDeps.include`; reran `npm run lint`, `npm test`, and `npm run build` successfully.

error_message: Entry actions menu failed to open in Storybook interaction coverage after trigger-level event propagation was stopped.
root_cause: Base UI Menu.Trigger needs its trigger event flow intact. Stopping pointer/click propagation on the trigger prevented the menu item from appearing.
fix: Removed trigger-level propagation stops, kept menu-item propagation isolation, switched trigger open styling to `data-popup-open`, disabled all entry actions during in-flight delete, and added Storybook coverage for opening Delete journal without selecting the row.

error_message: Sidebar nested entry rows and their ellipsis actions ended too far inside the sidebar because submenu nesting applied right margin and padding.
root_cause: `SidebarMenuSub` used symmetric horizontal margin/padding (`mx-*` and `px-*`), so each nested entry row lost right-side width along with left-side indentation.
fix: Changed submenu layout to left-only indentation (`ml-4 pl-2`) with no right margin/padding, pinned entry actions with `right-0`, and added Storybook coverage for the right-edge class plus async menu opening.

error_message: Horizontal split line between Search and entries rendered outside the sidebar bounds.
root_cause: The added `SidebarSeparator` used Base UI Separator's `data-horizontal:w-full`; adding horizontal margins made it overflow past the sidebar width.
fix: Removed the extra separator element and used the scoped `SidebarHeader` bottom border as the split line, with a stronger sidebar-border color. Browser check confirmed zero sidebar separator elements and header bounds inside the sidebar.

error_message: The new group plus button for Journal/Note was visible but the folder row trigger intercepted pointer clicks in the browser.
root_cause: The first implementation positioned the group plus over a full-width collapsible trigger. Even with layering, the trigger could intercept browser/user pointer action over the plus slot.
fix: Changed the group header into a flex row where the collapsible trigger uses the remaining width and the plus button owns its own end slot. Browser payload verification confirmed Add Journal creates `journal`, top New Note from a selected journal creates `journal`, and Add Note creates `note`.

error_message: Next/Webpack failed to compile after the sidebar file was split into a folder: `Failed to read source code from components/journal/entry-sidebar.jsx`.
root_cause: The refactor deleted `components/journal/entry-sidebar.jsx` while existing extensionless imports and the running compiler still resolved that module path.
fix: Moved sidebar code and stories to `components/sidebar/*`, removed the `entry-sidebar` folder and index barrel, and updated app imports to explicit files; verified `npm run lint`, `npm run build-storybook`, and `npm run build`.

error_message: `DELETE /api/folders/:id` returned 500 when deleting a folder with entries or child folders.
root_cause: Folder delete only deleted the target folder row. Postgres foreign-key restricts blocked deletion when entries or child folders still referenced it, and the API surfaced that as a 500.
fix: Changed folder delete to collect the folder subtree, delete entries in those folders, delete child folders deepest-first in one transaction, and remove the same subtree from client state after success.

error_message: New file title kept reselecting the whole title after each typed character, so typing replaced the previous character instead of building the title.
root_cause: The editor focus effect depended on `title` and `body`, so the `title-all` focus target reran after every controlled title update.
fix: Kept latest title/body values in refs and made the focus effect depend only on `focusTarget`; expanded Storybook coverage to type over `Untitled 1` and assert the full typed title remains.

error_message: Folder row add/options buttons sat outside the row hover highlight while file options stayed inside the highlighted row.
root_cause: Folder rows rendered the collapsible trigger, add menu, and options menu as separate flex siblings, so the hover background only covered the trigger width.
fix: Changed the folder row to a relative full-width trigger with right padding and placed add/options in an absolute right-side slot inside that row; added Storybook geometry checks for action bounds.

error_message: Empty folders rendered an empty collapsible subtree when expanded, creating visible space with no children.
root_cause: `SidebarEntryGroup` always rendered `CollapsibleContent` and `SidebarMenuSub` even when the folder had no entries, child folders, draft, or active creation state.
fix: Render the collapsible subtree only when there is actual child content or an active create/draft state; added Storybook coverage that clicking an empty folder does not create a nested menu.

error_message: `npm run build` failed because Next 16.2.6 defaulted to Turbopack build and reported `Turbopack is not supported on this platform (darwin/arm64) because native bindings are not available`.
root_cause: The local install only loaded Next WASM bindings, while Turbopack production build requires native bindings on this platform. Next's local docs say Webpack can be selected with `next build --webpack`.
fix: Changed the build script to `next build --webpack`, matching the existing `next dev --webpack` path; verified `npx next build --webpack` passed before the script change.

error_message: Browser verification against `http://127.0.0.1:3001` showed the editor shell without a mounted CodeMirror editor, and the dev server logged `Blocked cross-origin request to Next.js dev resource /_next/webpack-hmr from "127.0.0.1"`.
root_cause: Next.js 16 blocks dev resources requested from origins other than the hostname used to start the server. The dev server was started as `localhost`, while browser verification opened `127.0.0.1`.
fix: Added `allowedDevOrigins: ["127.0.0.1"]` so local browser automation can use either localhost hostname after the dev server restarts.

error_message: Inactive Markdown headings showed a small left indent, list bullets had too much marker gap, and heading/list vertical spacing felt loose in the CodeMirror editor.
root_cause: The tree decoration refactor hid only `HeaderMark` hashes while leaving the following heading whitespace visible; inactive bullet replacement did not consume the original marker whitespace, and CSS added extra heading bottom padding/list left padding.
fix: Inactive heading decorations now hide the full marker prefix including following whitespace, empty ATX headings do not receive heading line sizing, inactive bullet replacements consume marker whitespace, and editor CSS uses tighter heading padding, fixed bullet width, and no extra list-line left padding.

error_message: Inactive inline Markdown formatting still showed raw delimiter marks such as `**bold**` even though the text was styled bold.
root_cause: Inline syntax markers used the visible `cm-markdown-syntax-marker` decoration unconditionally, unlike headings and list markers which hide or replace syntax on inactive lines.
fix: Inline Markdown syntax markers now use hidden decorations when their line is inactive and visible syntax decorations only on the active line; browser smoke confirmed `**` markers compute to `display: none`.

error_message: Markdown headings did not render correctly: inactive heading lines still showed raw `#` marker text and heading content stayed at body-size text.
root_cause: The inactive heading marker used a hidden mark decoration, but CodeMirror syntax highlighting still rendered a visible heading span for the same `#` prefix. Heading text also relied on line decoration sizing, which did not appear in the rendered line classes during browser verification.
fix: Inactive heading prefixes now use replace decorations that remove the raw marker text, and heading token classes carry their own font sizes so `# H1`, `## H2`, and `### H3` render as `H1`, `H2`, and `H3` at heading sizes.

error_message: When the desktop sidebar was closed, the fixed expand-sidebar button could overlap the journal breadcrumb in the top bar.
root_cause: The custom collapsed sidebar button was fixed at the top-left of the viewport, outside the top app bar layout flow.
fix: Moved CollapsedSidebarButton into the top app bar header row as an inline flex item beside the breadcrumb and removed the fixed overlay from AppSidebar.

error_message: Next dev warned that `@next/swc-darwin-arm64` and the fallback native binding were not installed, then skipped `.next/dev/lock` because WASM bindings were loaded.
root_cause: The native SWC optional dependency directories existed but contained only package metadata, not `next-swc.darwin-arm64.node`. `npm cache verify` also failed because `~/.npm` contains root-owned files, so normal npm cache repair is still blocked.
fix: Removed the broken generated SWC package folders and ran `npm install --cache /private/tmp/innerscript-npm-cache`; npm reinstalled `@next/swc-darwin-arm64` and `next-swc.darwin-arm64.node` now loads.

error_message: Editor footer metadata and word count could overlap the main CodeMirror editor area.
root_cause: The editor shell used a scrolling main container plus a `min-h-full` inner column, while CodeMirror enforced a fixed `512px` minimum height. The footer used `mt-auto`, so constrained heights could let the editor body consume footer space instead of scrolling above it.
fix: Changed the editor page to an overflow-hidden flex column, placed CodeMirror inside a `min-h-0 flex-1 overflow-y-auto` editor area, made the metadata row a `shrink-0` footer, and removed the fixed CodeMirror min-height in favor of `height: 100%`/`min-height: 100%`.

error_message: Integration tests failed with `Cannot find package 'server-only' imported from db/client.js` even though `server-only` was present in package.json and package-lock.json.
root_cause: The local `node_modules` install was incomplete after repo updates; the declared `server-only` dependency was missing on disk.
fix: Ran `npm install`, which restored `node_modules/server-only` without changing package.json or package-lock.json.

error_message: Full `npm test` failed in Storybook tests with `ReferenceError: note is not defined` after the entry type rename.
root_cause: The broad `document` to `note` text replacement accidentally changed DOM test references from `document.body` to `note.body`.
fix: Changed the affected Storybook test queries back to `document.body`; full `npm test` then passed 13 files and 53 tests.

error_message: `npm run build` failed with `EACCES: permission denied, unlink '.next/server/_instrument_lib_db_index_js.js'`, including when rerun outside the sandbox.
root_cause: The generated `.next` artifact remains owned or permissioned so the current user cannot unlink it during Next build cleanup.
fix: Not fixed in this session; this is the existing local `.next` ownership blocker. Lint, full tests, and focused integration tests passed, but build remains blocked until `.next` is removed or ownership is repaired outside the current constraints.
